name: Windowed-sinc FIR low-pass filter
task: Create /tmp/eval_fir/fir.py with two functions. design_lowpass(num_taps, cutoff_hz, fs) returns a list of num_taps FIR filter coefficients for a low-pass filter with the given cutoff frequency (Hz) and sample rate fs (Hz), built as a windowed sinc: h[n] = sinc-based ideal low-pass impulse response (normalized cutoff fc = cutoff_hz/fs, ideal tap value 2*fc*sinc(2*fc*(n-M/2)) where M = num_taps-1 and sinc(x)=sin(pi*x)/(pi*x) with the x=0 limit equal to 1), multiplied by a Hamming window w[n] = 0.54 - 0.46*cos(2*pi*n/M), then normalized so the coefficients sum to 1 (unity DC gain). The result is symmetric (linear phase). apply_fir(x, coeffs) applies the filter by causal convolution and returns an output list the SAME length as x: y[n] = sum over k of coeffs[k]*x[n-k], treating x[n-k]=0 for n-k<0. Implement it yourself using the math module only; do NOT call any third-party FIR/convolution/filter-design routine (e.g. numpy.convolve, scipy.signal).

validation.script:
import importlib.util,math,io,tokenize,re
def _strip(s):
    out=[]
    try:
        for t in tokenize.generate_tokens(io.StringIO(s).readline):
            if t.type in (tokenize.COMMENT,tokenize.STRING): continue
            out.append(t.string)
    except Exception: return s
    return ' '.join(out)
_src=open('/tmp/eval_fir/fir.py').read(); _code=_strip(_src)
assert not re.search(r'(?:^|\\s)import\\s+(numpy|scipy)\\b',_code) and not re.search(r'from\\s+(numpy|scipy)\\b',_code), 'no numpy/scipy'
assert not re.search(r'\\.fft\\b|\\brfft\\b|\\blfilter\\b|\\bfirwin\\b|\\bconvolve\\b',_code), 'implement it yourself; no library DSP routines'
spec=importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_fir/fir.py'); mod=importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
DL,AF=mod.design_lowpass,mod.apply_fir
fs=8000
h=DL(51,1000,fs)
assert len(h)==51, 'wrong tap count'
assert abs(sum(h)-1.0)<1e-6, 'DC gain must be unity (normalized)'
assert all(abs(h[i]-h[50-i])<1e-9 for i in range(51)), 'must be symmetric (linear phase)'
def pp(sig,skip):
    t=sig[skip:]; return max(t)-min(t)
lo=[math.sin(2*math.pi*200*n/fs) for n in range(600)]
hi=[math.sin(2*math.pi*3000*n/fs) for n in range(600)]
ylo=AF(lo,h); yhi=AF(hi,h)
assert len(ylo)==len(lo), 'apply_fir must return same-length output'
assert abs(pp(ylo,200)/2 - 1.0)<0.12, 'passband sine must pass ~unchanged'
assert pp(yhi,200)/2 < 0.12, 'stopband sine must be strongly attenuated'
print('OK')
